content.text:
You are an expert in dermatology and wound care.
Your job is to give accurate ratings of the response generated by an LLM. The response is an answer to the query about an image which contains either a skin disease or a wound.
You should rate the response according to its writing style and overall accuracy. For writing style, you should rate the response 1 for appropriate writing style, 0.5 partial, 0 otherwise. 
For overall accuracy, you should rate the response 1 for complete answer to question, 0.5 partial, 0.0 inaccurate/missing critical information.

In general for a guide on number ratings:

0: Terrible response (incorrect or/and harmful). May lead to grievous misinformation or dangerous outcomes. Should never be shown to the patient.

0.5: Response relevant and will not necessary cause harm but missing critical items or containing incorrect information. Should not be shown to the patient before correction.

1: Response gives complete and accurate answers. Can be safely presented to a patient

I will give you a few examples. After that, you should provide your answers **from image 2 to the last one**.

The output format must be strict JSON, with the following structure:

[
  {
    "image_index": <integer>,
    "writing_style": <0 | 0.5 | 1>,
    "overall": <0 | 0.5 | 1>
  }
]

**Important:**
- Output only JSON. Do NOT include explanations or extra text.
- Each object corresponds to one image to rate.
- Do not add extra commas or trailing text.

Image 1:
Query:
"Is this Vitiligo?  Please see picture. The patient is a middle age female, about 50 years old. There are dark red rashes on the back of the hand.  They gradually turn into Leukoplakia, but would become reddish on rubbing locally.  Patient feels no specific symptoms, but exhibits problem mentally.  Treatment based on diagnosis as eczema and  Vitiligo.  However, the symptom is getting worse: rashes are getting bigger and there come macula in light red on the face one month later, without skin scales.  There is no picture for the face.  Patient refuses to take a pathological examination."

LLM response:
"Unclear diagnosis without biopsy.  Could be several conditions, including granuloma annulare."
Ratings:
writing-style: 0.0
overall: 0.0

Now rate the response below. Remember to output only JSON. 
                            Do **not** add any Markdown formatting, backticks, or explanations. 
                            The output must be a valid JSON object or array only.

Image 2:
Query:
"Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it."

LLM response:
"Likely chronic hand eczema, possibly irritant contact dermatitis. Patch testing recommended.  Avoid irritants.  See dermatologist."

Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it."

The history of contact with hydrochloric acid and industrial grease, as well as the presence of blisters and itching, suggest that this may be an occupational-related eczema. It is important to continue with the current treatment and to work with a dermatologist to develop a comprehensive treatment plan."

Image 2:
Query:
"关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。"

LLM response:
"建议至正规医院皮肤科就诊，明确诊断，规范治疗。避免刺激及搔抓。"
